narrative: Jindal Stainless remained the energy leader on absolute total energy, non-renewable energy, non-renewable electricity, non-renewable fuel and physical intensity. Its total energy was 35.14 million GJ and physical intensity was 19.97 GJ/tcs, still materially below Tata at 28.3 GJ/tcs, JSW at 23.79 GJ/tcs and SAIL at 32 GJ/tcs. JSW led renewable energy at 1.98 million GJ and renewable electricity at 1.85 million GJ, indicating stronger renewable scaling than Tata. Tata improved total and non-renewable energy versus FY2023 but ranked only 3rd on total energy and physical intensity. SAIL was the weakest on total energy and physical intensity, and its non-renewable electricity value is marked standardised/corrected, requiring caution. Jindal’s BRSR indicates PAT overachievement at Jajpur and Hisar, supporting its strong intensity position.
